returned.attacked_work: The upstream brick-qa-execution-permission-hardening-0625-work (agent-object:dev, adapter:codex-local) change: adapter_local_cli.py adds `allowed_tools` to `_claude_cli_invocation` knobs and emits `--allowedTools` in the claude argv; kernel_checks.py extends `_agent_effective_write_probe` to capture the claude write argv via a mock runner and assert `--tools`, `--allowedTools`, allowedTools==tools, and Bash pre-allowed. Attacked against the declared contract: 'fix or precisely classify the Claude QA execution-permission gap; add a checker proving intended behavior OR explicit limitation; QA-with-write_scope can run a harmless checker, OR route execution-QA off claude-local'.
boundary_violations: No protocol-law boundary violation observed in the diff: edits stay within declared write_scope (support/connection/*.py, support/checkers/lib/*.py); no AGENTS.md/.git/credential edits; adapter remains a capability connection (YAML/Brick/tool-policy still own authority); no success/quality/Movement field added to knobs.

Scope-of-task observation (not a law violation): the building offered two acceptance paths — (a) make claude execution-QA actually work, or (b) enforce routing so execution-QA does NOT use claude-local (claude read-only). Only path (a)'s argv emission + an argv-shape checker was delivered; no preset/routing enforcement was added and no live execution proof exists. Whether argv-shape proof + the live-still-gated symptom in this very QA step is SUFFICIENT to close the P0 'QA without execution verification is unacceptable' is a sufficiency call for the Link gate, not a QA verdict.
evidence_used: git diff (HEAD) of support/checkers/lib/kernel_checks.py and support/connection/adapter_local_cli.py

live shell observations: `claude --help`; gated `python3`/`uv run python3`/`check_profile.py --profile agent_axis_behavioral`/`git --version`/`ps`; passing `ls`/`git diff --check`/`git status`/`git rev-parse`
failing_or_missing_probes: MISSING (blocked): live execution of `support/checkers/check_profile.py --profile agent_axis_behavioral` to confirm the upstream EXIT-0 claim — blocked by provider approval gate on python in this claude-local QA session.

MISSING (blocked): compile/import smoke (`py_compile`) on the two changed files — blocked by the same approval gate (`python3`/`uv` gated). Static read shows imports resolve and the argv code is consistent, but no execution proof.

MISSING (inherently un-runnable in checker harness): a probe proving the LIVE provider honors `--allowedTools ...,Bash` by actually running a harmless shell command without approval. The delivered checker asserts argv SHAPE only; the building's task offered 'QA adapter with write_scope can run a harmless command/checker' as an acceptance path, and that live-behavior proof is absent.
negative_probe_observations: Negative probe (live): the declared target checker `check_profile.py --profile agent_axis_behavioral` could NOT be executed from this claude-local QA step — provider returned 'requires approval'. The fix being present in the worktree source did NOT make the running claude QA session able to execute python/checkers.

Negative probe (selective gating): `git diff`/`git status`/`ls` pass while `git --version`/`python3`/`uv` are gated. This selective pattern is consistent with a COMMAND-SPECIFIC allowlist (e.g. Bash(git diff:*)), not a bare `Bash` allowlist. If a bare-`Bash` `--allowedTools` entry (which the fix emits) fully pre-allowed Bash, `python3` would also pass — so whether the fix's bare-`Bash` allowlist actually suppresses per-command approval in non-interactive `-p` is UNCONFIRMED by this live session (and the session may simply predate the patch).

Negative probe (provenance): could not read my own launch argv — `ps axww` and `$PPID` expansion are themselves approval-gated — so I cannot distinguish 'my session was launched without the patched argv' from 'patched argv is insufficient'.
not_proven: Whether emitting `--allowedTools <toolset incl Bash>` actually suppresses the non-interactive claude per-command approval gate (live provider behavior — not executable in checker; not reproducible via my own session which may predate the patch).
The upstream claim that `agent_axis_behavioral` runs EXIT 0 — I could not execute the checker (python approval-gated in this QA step).
Compile/import correctness of the two changed files beyond static read — py_compile was approval-gated.
Whether my own QA session was launched with or without the patched `--allowedTools` argv (process inspection gated).
Full regression coverage across all checker profiles (only agent_axis_behavioral wiring inspected, not run).
Semantic correctness of the implementation and support loader behavior (explicitly out of QA proof reach).
Whether the delivered argv-shape proof is sufficient to close the P0 contract, or whether routing-policy enforcement was required — a Link-gate sufficiency decision, not a QA verdict.

observed_evidence.own_probes: QA-built probes distinct from the builder's mock-runner fixture: (1) executed the real target checker command end-to-end through my Bash tool (gated); (2) executed `claude --help` and confirmed `--allowedTools, --allowed-tools <tools...>` and `--tools <tools...>` are real, distinct flags (allowlist vs available-set); (3) statically traced `_run_or_delegate` -> `_command_runner_accepts_env` to confirm the new keyword-only `env` mock runner is invoked safely; (4) grepped all 7 `_claude_cli_invocation` call sites for exact-dict/key-set equality that the added `allowed_tools` key could break.
observed_evidence.real_entry_surface: Real caller surface driven: my own claude-local QA Bash tool (the same provider approval plane the symptom is about) AND the declared checker verb `support/checkers/check_profile.py --profile agent_axis_behavioral`. Observed output for the checker verb: 'This command requires approval' (non-zero, never executed). Observed output for `claude --help`: lines for `--allowedTools, --allowed-tools <tools...>`, `--tools <tools...>`, `--permission-mode <mode>` present.
observed_evidence.symptom_reproduction: In THIS code-attack-qa step (selected_adapter_ref=adapter:claude-local, native_grant.write_effective=true, tool-policy:read-write-scoped, declared write_scope present), I drove my own Bash tool surface and the reported symptom reproduced live: `python3 -m py_compile ...`, `python3 -c "print('probe')"`, `uv run python3 -m py_compile ...`, and the building's own target checker `uv run python3 support/checkers/check_profile.py --profile agent_axis_behavioral` ALL returned 'This command requires approval'. `git --version`, `ps axww`, `$PPID` expansion, and compound (`;`/`&&`) commands were likewise approval-gated. By contrast `ls`, `git diff --check`, `git diff --stat`, `git status --short`, `git rev-parse`, and `pwd` ran without approval. This is the exact reported failure mode: execution-QA via claude-local cannot run python/checker commands despite acceptEdits + write_scope.

observed_evidence: git diff --check on the two changed files produced no output (no whitespace/conflict markers).

support/checkers/profiles/agent_axis_behavioral.yaml line 14 lists kernel_check `agent_adapter_return_shape`; kernel_checks.py:2403 `run_agent_adapter_return_shape` calls `_agent_effective_write_probe` (line 2455) which contains the new --allowedTools argv assertions, so the new probe is statically wired into the profile.

adapter_local_cli.py `_claude_cli_invocation` (lines 836-905) now returns `allowed_tools` in all three branches (scoped-write=tools incl Bash; read-tier=read tools; plan=''), and `_invoke_local_cli` (lines 702-704) emits `--allowedTools <allowed_tools>` only when non-empty.
returned.output_excerpt: I now have a thorough, execution-grounded picture. Let me compile the final QA evidence return. Key findings from my independent attack: - **Static**: the fix is internally consistent; `--allowedTools`/`--allowed-tools <tools...>` confirmed real via `claude --help`; new checker is wired into `agent_axis_behavioral` via `agent_adapter_return_shape`; all symbols resolve; `_run_or_delegate`'s `_command_runner_accepts_env` makes the keyword-only `env` mock runtime-safe; no regression to the other 5 `_claude_cli_invocation` callers (all use targeted key access). - **Live (own QA session, the rea...
proof_limits: workspace write is limited by Brick-declared write_scope
regression_risks: Low for the added knob key: all 7 `_claude_cli_invocation` callers (adapter_local_cli.py:702; case_runners.py:6753,6785; kernel_checks.py:1129,1203,1390,1545) use targeted key access (`.get`/`["tools"]`/`["permission_mode"]`), none asserts exact dict or key-set equality, so the extra `allowed_tools` key does not break existing assertions (verified by read).

`--allowedTools <tools...>` is a variadic flag receiving a single comma-joined token followed by `--`-prefixed flags or the final prompt; commander stops variadic at the next option, mirroring the pre-existing `--tools` handling — no greedy-consumption regression observed statically, but not executed live.

The new checker's mock runner declares keyword-only `env`; safe only because `_run_or_delegate` gates the `env=` call behind `_command_runner_accepts_env` (introspects co_varnames). If that introspection helper were ever changed to pass `env` positionally, this probe would raise TypeError. Coupling noted, not currently broken.

observed_evidence: Building qa-execution-permission-hardening-0625 ran two carried Bricks: work (agent-object:dev, adapter:codex-local) then code-attack-qa (agent-object:qa, adapter:claude-local), forwarded to this closure via link-row edge code-attack-qa -> closure.

DEV step-output (work-attempt-1) reports made_changes=true, changed_files=[support/checkers/lib/kernel_checks.py, support/connection/adapter_local_cli.py]; root cause classified as Claude adapter invocation projection: argv emitted --tools (incl Bash) but omitted Claude's --allowedTools approval allowlist, so Bash/python/checker could stay approval-gated in non-interactive Claude QA.

Source corroboration (closure read-only): adapter_local_cli.py:702-704 emits --allowedTools <allowed_tools> only when non-empty; _claude_cli_invocation (836-905) returns allowed_tools in all three branches (scoped-write tools incl Bash; read-tier read tools; plan='').

QA step-output (code-attack-qa-attempt-1) reproduced the live symptom INSIDE its own claude-local write-effective QA step: python3 -m py_compile, python3 -c, uv run python3, and the target verb check_profile.py --profile agent_axis_behavioral ALL returned 'This command requires approval'; ls/git diff --check/git status/git rev-parse/pwd ran without approval.

QA confirmed via live `claude --help` that --tools (available set) and --allowedTools/--allowed-tools (approval allowlist) are real, distinct flags; reported no protocol-law boundary violation in the diff (edits within declared write_scope; no AGENTS.md/.git/credential edits; no success/quality/Movement field added).
